title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dark circles
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dark circles are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to treat various skin conditions, including dark circles under the eyes. They are a natural and effective alternative to chemical-based treatments. Some popular herbal tinctures for dark circles include eyebright, bilberry, and licorice root. Eyebright reduces inflammation and irritation, bilberry improves blood flow and reduces the appearance of dark circles, and licorice root has skin-lightening properties that help to reduce dark circles. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can improve their appearance and boost their confidence, leading to a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with dark circles because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes. The tincture is made by soaking arnica flowers in a solvent, such as alcohol or glycerin, to extract the active compounds. These compounds include helenalin and dihydrohelenalin, which have been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. By improving circulation, arnica flower tincture can help eliminate toxins and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, the tincture's anti-oxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate dark circles caused by swollen blood vessels and puffiness around the eyes. The main active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones, such as helenalin and dihydrohelenalin.

Vasoconstrictor: Arnica flower tincture constricts blood vessels, which can diminish the appearance of dark circles by decreasing the visibility of dilated blood vessels under the delicate skin around the eyes. The flavonoids and tannins in arnica contribute to its vasoconstrictive properties.

Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the development of dark circles. The phenolic acids, flavonoids, and volatile oils in arnica exhibit strong antioxidant activities.

Improves microcirculation: Arnica flower tincture enhances local microcirculation, promoting better blood flow and oxygenation in the affected area, which can help diminish the appearance of dark circles. The saponins and essential oils in arnica are believed to be responsible for its microcirculatory effects.

Skin-regenerating: Arnica flower tincture supports skin regeneration and healing, which can help improve the overall appearance of the skin around the eyes and reduce the visibility of dark circles. The helenalin derivatives, flavonoids, and essential oils in arnica contribute to its skin-regenerating properties.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The arnica flower contains flavonoids and volatile oils that can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it effective for reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant have similar properties to the flowers and can be used as a gentle astringent to tone and brighten the skin around the eyes.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the arnica plant contain helenalin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for reducing puffiness and discoloration associated with dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried arnica flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the arnica flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the resulting arnica flower tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and keep it in a cool, dark place; it will last for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tincture helps with dark circles because it contains anti-inflammatory and brightening properties that can reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. The active compounds in daisy, such as flavonoids and tannins, help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to decrease puffiness and discoloration. Additionally, daisy has natural skin-lightening properties that can help to fade dark circles and promote a more even skin tone. Its soothing and calming effects also help to reduce redness and irritation, leaving the skin looking refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in daisies.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of daisy tincture help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dark circles. This is attributed to the high levels of phenolic acids found in daisies.

Brightening: Daisy tincture has natural brightening properties that can help even out skin tone and reduce the appearance of dark circles. This is largely due to the presence of taraxasterol, a compound found in daisies that inhibits melanin production.

Astringent: Daisy tincture has mild astringent properties that can help tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of dark circles. This is because of the tannins present in daisies, which have a constricting effect on blood vessels.

Soothing: Daisy tincture's soothing properties can help alleviate irritation and redness, which can exacerbate the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of daisies, which help calm and protect the skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, daisy flowers help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and tannins, which strengthen blood vessels and decrease dark circle appearance.
Buds: High in phenolic acids, daisy buds aid in reducing skin irritation and hyperpigmentation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh daisy flowers, filling a clean jar about 3/4 full.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged, covering with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out all liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with dark circles because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the skin around the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help strengthen blood vessels and improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, pot marigold has been shown to have soothing and calming effects on the skin, which can help reduce puffiness and irritation. When applied topically, the tincture can help hydrate and nourish the delicate skin under the eyes, promoting a brighter and more even complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate dark circles caused by puffy eyes or skin irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this property include lutein and zeaxanthin.

Venotonic: Pot marigold tincture helps improve circulation and strengthen blood vessel walls, reducing dark circles caused by poor circulation or leaky capillaries. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and essential oils.

Astringent: The astringent properties of pot marigold tincture help tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by loose or sagging skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Emollient: Pot marigold tincture has moisturizing properties that help soothe and soften the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by dryness or irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include waxes and sterols.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have a soothing effect on the skin and can help improve blood circulation, reducing dark circles.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils that have moisturizing properties and can help improve the skin's overall health, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh pot marigold flowers (Calendula officinalis) and let them wilt for 24 hours to reduce their water content.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with dark circles because it contains bioflavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These compounds can help reduce puffiness and tighten the skin around the eyes, making dark circles less noticeable. Additionally, eyebright tincture is believed to improve blood circulation and strengthen capillary walls, which can help prevent the pooling of blood beneath the skin that can cause dark circles. Furthermore, eyebright tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture reduces inflammation and swelling, helping to alleviate dark circles caused by fluid build-up and inflammation in the eye area. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides.

Astringent: Eyebright tincture tightens and tones the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation. Tannins and phenolic acids in eyebright contribute to its astringent properties.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tincture protects the skin from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of dark circles. Flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamins A and C in eyebright provide its antioxidant benefits.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture helps to prevent and treat eye infections that can contribute to dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, phenolic acids, and volatile oils.

Lymphatic system support: Eyebright tincture promotes lymphatic drainage, reducing fluid build-up and congestion in the eye area, which can help alleviate dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers of eyebright tincture plant help reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the leaves of eyebright tincture plant soothe irritated skin and help diminish dark circles.
Stems: High in tannins, the stems of eyebright tincture plant provide astringent properties that tighten and firm the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb and place it in a clean jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle and use a few drops on a cotton ball to apply to dark circles as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The tannins in witch hazel act as astringents, tightening the skin and shrinking dilated blood vessels that can cause dark circles. Additionally, its soothing and healing properties can alleviate any irritation or redness, providing a brighter and more even complexion. The natural ingredients in herbal witch hazel tincture make it a gentle and effective solution for improving the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by underlying inflammation or skin irritation. This property is due to the presence of tannins and gallic acid.

Astringent: Witch hazel tincture tightens and tones the skin, which can help minimize the appearance of dark circles by constricting blood vessels and reducing puffiness. This is also attributed to the tannins and gallic acid content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture contains antioxidants that protect the skin from damage and help maintain skin health, which can indirectly contribute to reducing dark circles. The primary antioxidant compounds are proanthocyanidins, gallic acid, and vitamin C.

Anti-bacterial: Witch hazel tincture has antibacterial properties that can help maintain skin cleanliness and prevent infections, which can exacerbate dark circles. The main antibacterial constituents are tannins, gallic acid, and volatile oils.

Venotonic: Witch hazel tincture strengthens and supports blood vessel health, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by weakened or dilated blood vessels. This is primarily due to the presence of rutin, a bioflavonoid that supports capillary integrity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, witch hazel flowers help reduce inflammation and strengthen blood vessels, which can alleviate dark circles.
Leaves: Containing tannins and volatile oils, witch hazel leaves can soothe and tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant has astringent properties due to its high tannin content, making it effective in diminishing dark circles and puffiness.
Twigs: Packed with flavonoids and saponins, twigs of the witch hazel plant can improve blood flow and reduce dark circles caused by poor circulation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried witch hazel bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum over the chopped bark, ensuring it is fully submerged, then seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out any excess liquid from the bark.
Store the final tincture in a cool, dark place for up to a year, and apply a small amount to a cotton ball to use on dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with dark circles because it contains hypericin and hyperforin, two compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels under the skin, which can help to diminish the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to have a positive effect on the health of the skin, promoting collagen production and improving skin elasticity. This can help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles around the eyes, further contributing to a more youthful and refreshed appearance. Furthermore, St. John's wort has been used traditionally as a remedy for various skin conditions, including wounds, burns and bruises, due to its astringent and vulnerary properties.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by swollen blood vessels and skin irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antioxidant: The tincture contains antioxidants that protect the skin from free radical damage, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of dark circles. Active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Antidepressant: St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and depression, which can contribute to dark circles. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is hypericin.

Venotonic: The tincture helps improve circulation and strengthens blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by visible blood vessels. Active constituents responsible for this property include rutin and hesperidin.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent and treat skin infections, promoting overall skin health and reducing the likelihood of dark circles caused by skin issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar compounds to the flowers, and when used in tea, may help improve blood flow and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
Buds: The buds of St. John's Wort are a concentrated source of active compounds, and when used in tea, can provide a stronger effect in reducing dark circles and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar about 3/4 full.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the herbs until they are completely covered, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Cap the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out all the liquid.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. It contains rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the skin, which can help to reduce stress-related dark circles. Its mild astringent properties can also help to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Overall, lemon balm tincture can help to brighten and rejuvenate the eye area, leaving you looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm tincture help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Calming: Lemon balm tincture has calming properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can lead to improved circulation and reduced dark circles. This is due to the presence of citronellal and geraniol.

Vasoconstrictor: Lemon balm tincture has vasoconstrictor properties that can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the area. This is due to the presence of eugenol.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm tincture can help keep the skin around the eyes clean and free from infection, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of citral and linalool.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in rosmarinic acid, the leaves of lemon balm are used to make medicinal tea for dark circles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: High in eugenol, the flowers of lemon balm are used in medicinal tea to help reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes.
Stems: Containing essential oils, the stems of lemon balm are used in medicinal tea to promote circulation and alleviate dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean jar.
Add 1 cup of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring all leaves are submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid, and store tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Apply tincture to a cotton ball and gently dab under eyes twice daily to reduce dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with dark circles because it contains menthol, a natural vasoconstrictor that can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by constricting the blood vessels beneath the skin. Additionally, peppermint has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting circulation. This increased blood flow can help to nourish and oxygenate the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles and promoting a more refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its rosmarinic acid content, help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, a common cause of dark circles.
Vasoconstrictor: The menthol in peppermint tincture acts as a vasoconstrictor, which can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the area.
Antioxidant: Peppermint tincture contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which can help protect the delicate skin under the eyes from damage and darkening caused by free radicals.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of peppermint tincture, due to its menthol and menthone content, can help keep the area under the eyes clean and free from infection, preventing further darkening of the skin.

Cooling and soothing: Peppermint tincture has a cooling and soothing effect on the skin, which can help reduce puffiness and alleviate irritation, providing relief to the sensitive skin under the eyes and improving the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, peppermint leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tea for dark circles due to their skin-revitalizing properties.
Flowers: High in anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint flowers can help reduce puffiness and dark circles when used in medicinal tea.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and antioxidants, peppermint seeds can contribute to the overall effectiveness of medicinal tea for dark circles, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of fresh peppermint leaves and place them in a clean jar.
Pour 1 cup of vodka or rum over the peppermint leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture and discard the peppermint leaves, reserving the liquid.
Store the peppermint tincture in a dark glass bottle and use a few drops on a cotton pad to gently apply to dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can reduce the puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to improve microcirculation and decrease capillary permeability, which can help alleviate the appearance of dark circles caused by poor blood flow and fluid buildup. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the visibility of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like azulenes, help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, a common cause of dark circles.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including terpenoids and flavonoids, helps protect and repair skin damage, reducing the appearance of dark circles.

Vascular constriction: German chamomile tincture contains active compounds that cause vasoconstriction, reducing blood flow and minimizing the pooling of blood under the skin, which can cause dark circles.

Soothing and calming: Its soothing and calming properties, owed to active constituents like matricin and apigenin, help alleviate irritation and reduce redness, brightening the appearance of dark circles.

Skin regeneration: German chamomile tincture promotes skin regeneration and healing, addressing one of the root causes of dark circles – damaged or thin skin – by stimulating the production of new skin cells.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves can help soothe and relax the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Seeds: High in essential oils, chamomile seeds can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, lightening dark circles over time.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh German chamomile flowers, ideally in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Coarsely chop the flowers and place them in a clean glass jar, then add 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.

Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a small amount of the tincture to a cotton ball and gently dab it on the dark circles, avoiding the eye area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with dark circles because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as allantoin, aid in skin repair and regeneration, while its astringent properties help tighten and tone the skin. Additionally, plantain tincture has been known to improve blood circulation, which can further minimize the appearance of dark circles. Its soothing and cooling effects also provide relief to tired and irritated eyes, promoting a refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can lessen the appearance of dark circles caused by puffy eyes or skin irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.

Venotonic: Plantain tincture strengthens blood vessels, reducing their permeability and fragility, which can help minimize dark circles resulting from leaked blood and pooled fluid under the eyes. The rutin and other flavonoids present in plantain contribute to this property.

Antioxidant: Plantain tincture contains potent antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin under the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the formation of dark circles. The high levels of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins in plantain give it this antioxidant property.

Astringent: Plantain tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten and firm the skin under the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by loose or thin skin. The tannins in plantain are responsible for its astringent effects.

Soothing: Plantain tincture has soothing properties that can help alleviate skin irritation, redness, and itching, which can exacerbate dark circles. The mucilage, iridoid glycosides, and flavonoids in plantain contribute to its soothing effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain tincture plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for dark circles due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain tincture plant contain mucilage, a substance that can help soothe and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles.

Flowers: The flowers of the plantain tincture plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, both of which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantain leaves, rinse, and let dry for 12 hours.
Chop leaves into small pieces, fill a jar halfway, and add 80 proof vodka to cover leaves.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store tincture in a dark glass bottle and apply a few drops under the eyes daily to reduce dark circles.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dark circles with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of potent herbs known for their skin-lightening and circulatory benefits. A popular choice is a tincture made from calendula, eyebright, and licorice root. Calendula promotes skin cell regeneration, while eyebright reduces inflammation and discoloration. Licorice root inhibits melanin production, which helps fade dark circles. Mix 10-15 drops of this tincture with a carrier oil and gently massage the area around the eyes daily. Remember to store the tincture in a cool, dark place and always test it on a small patch of skin before applying it to the eye area.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dark circles that are treated with herbal tinctures are puffy eyes and under-eye bags. Eyebright and goldenseal are two herbs often used in tinctures for treating these conditions. Eyebright has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce puffiness and redness. Goldenseal, on the other hand, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and decrease swelling. Other herbs like chamomile and fennel are also used in tinctures to improve blood circulation, which can help minimize the appearance of dark circles. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
